LAW AND MOTION 302, THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. RESPONDENTS WILLIAM VICKREY, THE JUDICIAL COUNCIL OF CALIFORNIA AND THE ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICE OF THE COURTS' DEMURRER TO PETITIONER'S FIRST AMENDED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE UNDER CCP 1085 IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. CAL. GOV'T. CODE 71802 DOES NOT CONFER AN ENTITLEMENT TO A PERMANENT OPT-OUT STATUS AND PLAINTIFF FAILS TO PLEAD FACTS SHOWING THERE WAS ANOTHER RULE OR UNDERSTANDING CONFERRING SUCH ENTITLEMENT. THERE IS NO REASONABLE PROBABILITY THAT GRANTING LEAVE TO AMEND WOULD CURE THE DEFECTS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: JAMES J. MCBRIDE; COURT REPORTER: TEANNA WARD, CSR # 11918

DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Apr-16-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Suzanne I. Price, Esq., of Wiley Price & Radulovich, LLP, Tel. 510.337.2810, appearing for Respondent and moving party.
Jose Guerrerro, Tel. 415.641.6135, Petitioner and opposing party appearing on his own behalf.

The matter was argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. RESPONDENTS WILLIAM VICKREY, THE JUDICIAL COUNCIL OF CALIFORNIA and THE ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICE OF THE COURTS' DEMURRER TO PETITIONER'S FIRST AMENDED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE UNDER CCP 1085 is Sustained without leave to amend. Cal. Gov't. Code 71802 does not confer an entitlement to a permanent opt-out status and Plaintiff fails to plead facts showing there was another rule or understanding conferring such entitlement. There is no reasonable probability that granting leave to amend would cure the defects. Order signed in open court. Judge: James J. McBride; Court Reporter: Teanna Ward, CSR # 11918

DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Feb-17-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jose Guerrero, Tel. 415.641.6135, Petitioner and moving party appearing on his own behalf.
Suzanne I. Price, Esq., of Wiley Price & Radulovich, LLP, Tel. 510.337.2810, appearing for Respondent and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as modified. RESPONDENTS WILLIAM VICKREY, THE JUDICIAL COUNCIL OF CALIFORNIA AND THE ADMINSTRATIVE OFFICE OF THE COURTS' DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS sustained with 30 days leave to amend to allege due process. Respondents do not have an obligation to reinstate petitioner's opt out status. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077

Petition For Writ Of Mandate

MINI MINUTES:

First Amended Petition For Preemptory Writ Of Administrove Mandamus Ccp 1094. And Related Claims (Per Verbal Order Of Court On 10/31)
Off Calendar Dec-12-2011 Text Ruling

AMENDED DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Sep-16-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
RESPONDENTS WILLIAM VICKREY, THE ADMINSTRATIVE OFFICE OF THE COURTS, AND THE JUDICIAL COUNCIL OF CALIFORNIA'S REVISED DEMURRER TO PETITIONER'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF ADMINISTRATIVE MANDAMUS (C.C.P. SECTION 1094.5.

APPEARANCES: Jose Guerrero, 374-7089 petitioner & opposing party in propria persona; Suzanne I. Price, Esq. of Wiley Price & Radulovich, LLP 510/337-2810 for respondents & moving parties.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling as modified - SUSTAINED WITH 30 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE IN GOOD FAITH, A PROPER CLAIM UNDER CCP SECTION 1094.5 WHICH REQUIRES AN ADMINISTRATIVE DECISION MADE AS A RESULT OF A PROCEEDING IN WHICH A HEARING IS REQUIRED. Respondent to prepare a proposed form of order. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

Notice Of Motion For Peremptory Writ Of Adminstrative Mandamus
Off Calendar Sep-16-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PETITIONER JOSE GUERRERO'S MOTION FOR PEREMPTORY WRIT OF ADMINSTRATIVE MANDAMUS.

APPEARANCES: Jose Guerrero, 374-7089 petitioner & moving party in propria persona; Suzanne I. Price, Esq. of Wiley Price & Radulovich, LLP 510/337-2810 for respondents & opposing parties.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - OFF CALENDAR AS MOOT. In addition, the Court stated that it did not consider that taking the motion for peremptory writ off calendar to have in any way constituted a dismissal. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998
